is_humanoid_robotics.description: Relevance gate 1: true only if the paper is genuinely about humanoid / bipedal / legged ROBOTS. False for paleoanthropology, human anatomy/biomechanics, bioethics essays, pure computer-animation, or other non-robot uses of 'bipedal'/'humanoid'. If false, other fields may be left at their absence values.

is_engineering_research.description: Relevance gate 2: true only if this is an ENGINEERING / technical RESEARCH contribution about the robot itself (control, learning, hardware, perception, planning, systems). False for business/management/supply-chain/marketing studies, adoption/acceptance or trust surveys, policy/ethics/waste essays, promotional white papers, and forensic/anatomy studies — even when the abstract is tagged Engineering/CS and mentions humanoid robots. A review/survey OF robot technology is still engineering research (true); a market/adoption study is not (false).

task.description: Primary robotics task the paper addresses.

hardware_named.description: Named robot/hardware platform if any (e.g. 'Unitree G1', '19-DOF tendon hand'); null if none.

data_generated.description: Does the paper create/release a new dataset or corpus?

data_generated_desc.description: One-line description of the generated data (size/modality) if data_generated=true, else null.

benchmark_new.description: Does the paper propose a new benchmark/evaluation protocol?

maturity.description: TRL-proximate maturity of the demonstrated result.

openness.description: Code/data release stated in the abstract.

confidence.description: Extractor confidence given only the abstract.
